opening.title: About Tether
opening.tagline: Tether (n.) — an object that keeps something anchored to a point while allowing fluidity of motion.
opening.body: Our Tether is the digital version of that. Stay anchored to the people, rhythms, and responsibilities that matter most — then move fluidly within them. Life coordinated, not chaotic. And nothing important ever drifts.

sections.title: Household
sections.intro:
This is your daily functioning unit — the people you wake up with and go to bed knowing everything got handled. Who's picking the kids up from school. Who's grabbing groceries. Whether the lawn got mowed or the vacuuming got done. The small, invisible labor that keeps a home running.

Tasks are built exactly for this. Assign them, reassign them, pick them up when you have capacity. Tether keeps it all visible, shared, and moving — so nothing falls through the cracks and no one person carries it alone.

subsections.title: Recurring Tasks
subsections.body: Set a task to repeat daily, on specific weekdays, monthly, or annually. Tether automatically advances the due date each time you complete it.

subsections.title: Chore Lists
subsections.body: A checklist-style task with sub-items. Great for room-by-room cleaning, shared responsibilities, or anything with multiple steps. Tap All Done to reset and recur.

subsections.title: Grocery Lists
subsections.body: A smart shopping list with a built-in catalog. Items are grouped by department. Add custom items, adjust quantities, and mark items as you shop.

subsections.title: Joining a household
subsections.body: An admin invites you by email. You'll see a banner to accept or decline when you sign in. Joining is instant — no approval queue.

sections.title: Groups
sections.intro:
Some coordination happens beyond your front door. The extended family Thanksgiving where someone needs to claim the green bean casserole. The friend group Super Bowl party where half the people don't know each other but all need to know where to show up. Your buddy's 40th where twelve people are trying to figure out the plan in four different text threads.

Groups let you bring those people together in one place — invite everyone at once, coordinate who's bringing what, and make sure the right people have the right information with a few flicks of your thumb.

subsections.title: Invite in bulk
subsections.body: When creating an event, invite a whole group at once instead of adding people one by one. Individual group members can still be added separately.

subsections.title: Events
subsections.body: Create an event, invite people, and coordinate who's bringing what. Guests RSVP Going, Tentative, or Can't make it. Add a bring list so nothing gets doubled up.

subsections.title: Location
subsections.body: Add an address and it becomes a one-tap navigation link. Opens in Maps on any device.

subsections.title: Event Icons
subsections.body: Choose an icon to visually categorize your event — 🎂 birthday, ✈️ travel, 🏈 sports, and more. Icons are purely cosmetic — they help you scan your event list at a glance but don't change how events work.

sections.title: Contacts
sections.intro:
Not everyone needs to be in your household or a recurring group — but they still matter. The cousin you see twice a year. The neighbor who always comes to the block party. The friend you want to loop in for the next big thing. We have room for them too.

Household members and group members are automatically your contacts — no separate request needed. The spectrum from tight to loose: Household → Groups → Contacts.

subsections.title: Adding contacts
subsections.body: Send a contact request by email. They accept or decline. Once connected, you can invite them to events and see shared occasions.

subsections.title: Privacy
subsections.body: Contacts only see what you share with them. Nothing is visible by default — you choose what to make household-visible, contact-visible, or private.

sections.title: You
sections.intro:
Dealing with the ever-changing events in life and struggling to keep up — this is exactly what you've been missing. All of the above, and if you choose, a place to track and share the birthdays, anniversaries, and life occasions worth celebrating and remembering together.

Regardless of what you have going on in life, we're here to help you Get-Tethered.

subsections.title: One-Time Tasks
subsections.body: Due once. Swipe right to complete and dismiss it. Swipe left to snooze it to tomorrow.

subsections.title: Birthdays
subsections.body: Track birthdays for the people who matter. They surface on your Today tab on the day and in Beyond as they approach.

subsections.title: Anniversaries
subsections.body: Wedding anniversaries, work anniversaries, or any date worth marking each year. Optionally record the year it started.

subsections.title: Remembrance
subsections.body: Some dates are about honoring someone no longer here. A name and a date is enough — no Tether account required.
